35-week-plan.html.title: 35 Week Plan
35-week-plan.html.description:
Refeed The specifics for you: When: Your heaviest training day of the week. For most people that's leg day or the highest-volume upper day. Pick one day, keep it consistent.
Calories: ~3000 on refeed day (roughly maintenance for early phases, slightly below maintenance later as TDEE drops).
Protein: Same as always — 240g. Don't drop it.
The extra ~1200 cal vs your normal 1800 day: mostly carbs. Rice, pasta, oats, potatoes, fruit, bread. Not fats, not "treat foods."
Fat: Keep moderate. Don't make it a pizza day. Why carbs specifically: Refills muscle glycogen → better training performance the following days
Spikes leptin (the satiety/metabolism hormone that drops in a deficit) → blunts metabolic adaptation
Psychologically resets hunger and food obsession
Carbs are less calorie-dense than fat, so you can eat more food volume — which is part of the point What a refeed is NOT: ❌ A cheat day. "Refeed" ≠ "eat whatever you want." A 5000 cal binge undoes the entire week's deficit.
❌ Junk food day. Pizza, ice cream, takeaway — these can fit in moderation if tracked, but the goal is structured carbs, not a free-for-all.
❌ "Treat yourself" psychology. It's a metabolic and training tool, not a reward.
❌ Optional when motivation is low. If you skip refeeds because you "had a good week," you'll stall by week 8. Practical example refeed day for you: Breakfast: oats (80g) + banana + protein shake = ~500 cal, 35g protein
Lunch: rice (200g cooked) + chicken breast (200g) + veg = ~600 cal, 50g protein
Pre-workout: rice cakes + jam + protein shake = ~400 cal, 30g protein
Post-workout: pasta (150g dry) + lean mince + sauce = ~800 cal, 50g protein
Dinner: potato (300g) + fish + veg = ~500 cal, 40g protein
Evening: Greek yogurt + Zilch ice cream + powdered PB = ~250 cal, 35g protein ≈3050 cal, 240g protein, mostly carbs. That's a refeed.
Frequency in your plan: Weeks 1-2: No refeed. You're not deep enough yet.
Weeks 3-32: 1x per week, same day each week.
Week 10: The 5-day diet break replaces refeeds that week (you're at maintenance for 5 straight days).
Week 22: Same — 5-day break replaces refeeds.
Weeks 33-35: No refeed. Final push, deficit held tight. One warning:
The first few refeeds, the scale will jump 1-2kg the next morning. That's water and glycogen, not fat. Don't panic. Don't skip the next refeed because "it set me back." It didn't. By 3-4 days later the water is gone and you're lower than before.
This is exactly the daily-weight-noise pattern I'm not going to engage with. Refeed Saturday → scale up Sunday → I don't want to hear it. Trust the weekly average.
Hydration & Electrolytes
If you're training first thing, you don't want to start sipping creatine during the workout — you want some of it in you before. Two reasons: Electrolytes pre-workout = better performance. Sodium specifically helps with blood volume and pump. Going in dehydrated tanks lifts.
Creatine timing genuinely doesn't matter, but having it dissolved and ready means you actually drink it instead of forgetting. Suggested flow: Wake up: half a glass of plain water + metformin (if that's your morning dose schedule — confirm with your prescription).
Pre-gym (15-20 min before): 1/3 of the Owala. Gets electrolytes in, primes hydration.
During workout: sip another 1/3. Don't chug — sloshing stomach kills lifts.
Post-workout through afternoon: finish the rest.
Plain water on top: another 2-3L across the day. One thing about morning fasted training in a deficit:
You'll be lifting on essentially zero food the night before plus a sleep fast. Some people thrive on this, some crash. Watch for: Lightheadedness on heavy sets (especially leg day)
Strength noticeably lower than expected
Brain fog mid-workout If any of these hit consistently in week 1-2: have something small 30-45 min pre-lift. A protein shake + banana, or Greek yogurt + honey. ~200-300 cal. You account for it in your daily 1800 — it's not extra calories, it's redistributed timing.
Don't preemptively change this. Try fasted first. Most people are fine. Adjust only if you see the symptoms above. The plan is the plan.
Lifting Routine
PHASE 1 — WEEKS 1-2 (3x/week full body)
Goal: Build movement competency, manage soreness, establish habit.
Day A (Mon): Leg press: 3×10
Machine chest press: 3×10
Lat pulldown: 3×10
Machine shoulder press: 3×10
Leg curl: 3×12
Cable row: 3×10
Bicep curl machine: 2×12
Tricep pushdown: 2×12 Day B (Wed): Hack squat or leg press: 3×10
Incline machine press: 3×10
Machine row: 3×10
Leg extension: 3×12
Lateral raise machine: 3×12
Face pulls (cable): 3×15
Hammer curl: 2×12
Overhead tricep extension: 2×12 Day C (Fri): Leg press: 3×10
Machine chest press: 3×10
Lat pulldown: 3×10
Leg curl: 3×12
Machine shoulder press: 3×10
Cable row: 3×10
Calf raise: 3×15
Optional: ab work Weights: Start lighter than you think. Week 1 is "find the weight where I can hit 10 with 3 RIR." Week 2: add weight where week 1 felt easy. PHASE 2-4 — WEEKS 3-17 (4x/week upper-lower split)
This is your bread and butter for 15 weeks.
UPPER A (Mon): Machine chest press: 4×6-8
Lat pulldown: 4×8-10
Machine shoulder press: 3×8-10
Cable row: 3×10-12
Lateral raise machine: 3×12-15
Bicep curl machine: 3×10-12
Tricep pushdown: 3×10-12 LOWER A (Tue): Leg press: 4×6-8
Leg curl: 4×8-10
Leg extension: 3×10-12
Goblet squat or hack squat: 3×8-10
Calf raise: 4×12-15
Optional: ab machine 3×12 UPPER B (Thu): Incline machine press: 4×8-10
Machine row: 4×8-10
Lateral raise machine: 4×12-15
Lat pulldown (different grip): 3×10-12
Face pulls: 3×15
Hammer curl: 3×10-12
Overhead tricep extension: 3×10-12 LOWER B (Fri or Sat): Hack squat or leg press (heavier): 4×6-8
DB Romanian deadlift: 4×8-10
Leg curl: 3×10-12
Leg extension: 3×12-15
Calf raise: 4×12-15
Optional: ab work Progression rule: When you hit the top of the rep range across all working sets with good form, add weight next session. Example: chest press programmed 6-8 reps, you hit 8/8/8/8 on 60kg — next session, try 65kg for 6-7 reps and work back up. PHASE 5-6 — WEEKS 18-32 (4x/week, same split, intensity techniques added)
Same template, but on the last set of select exercises, add one of: Drop sets (e.g., chest press: hit failure at working weight, immediately drop ~30%, hit failure again). Use on 1-2 isolation exercises per session.
Rest-pause (hit failure, rest 15 sec, hit failure again, rest 15 sec, hit failure again). Use on machines, not free weights.
Myo-reps (similar to rest-pause but shorter rests, fewer reps). Advanced, optional. Don't apply intensity techniques to compounds (leg press, chest press as a primary). Save them for isolations (curls, extensions, lateral raises).
Why now: as calories drop and recovery shrinks, total volume needs to come down slightly. Intensity techniques compress more stimulus into less volume — efficient. PHASE 7 — WEEKS 33-35 (3-4x/week, dial back volume)
Final 3 weeks: protect what you've built, don't try to grow.
Option: Drop to upper-lower 3x/week (e.g., Upper Mon, Lower Wed, Upper Fri), or maintain 4x but reduce sets per exercise by 1. Reduce intensity techniques. No more drop sets.
Stay in 8-12 rep range mostly. Less heavy work, more pump-style.
If a lift feels off, skip it. Recovery is shot, ego doesn't matter. CARDIO STRUCTURE
You haven't actually been doing cardio in this plan — just steps. Steps are cardio, walking is the most underrated tool. But you green-lit adding zone-2 from week 18.
Steps (whole plan): Wk 1-2: 8,000/day
Wk 3-6: 10,000/day
Wk 7-17: 10,000-12,000/day
Wk 18-24: 14,000/day
Wk 25-35: 15,000/day How: walk to/from gym, walk during phone calls, walk after meals (also helps with metformin GI side effects), walking pad if you study sitting. Don't make it complicated.
Zone-2 cardio (weeks 18-35): 2x per week, 30 minutes
"Conversational pace" — heart rate roughly 60-70% of max (~115-135 bpm for you)
Modes: incline treadmill walk (best — low impact, easy to maintain zone), stationary bike, elliptical
NOT running. At your size + lifting, running adds joint stress and recovery debt. Walking incline gets the same metabolic effect with less cost. When to do cardio: Best: separate session from lifting (e.g., morning lift, evening walk)
Acceptable: after lifting, never before
Worst: same time as legs day. Skip it leg day. Why zone-2 specifically: Burns calories without crushing recovery
Improves cardiovascular base (you're a nursing student — fitness on the floor matters)
Doesn't trigger same hunger spike as HIIT
Sustainable for 18 weeks Updated weekly template (late cut, weeks 25+): WHAT TO EXPECT FROM YOUR LIFTS OVER 35 WEEKS Weeks 1-12: Strength up almost every session. Newbie gains. You'll add weight to the bar weekly. Enjoy it.
Weeks 13-20: Progress slows. PRs every 2-3 sessions instead of every session. Still gaining.
Weeks 21-28: Mostly maintaining. Some lifts still creep up, others stall. Normal.
Weeks 29-35: Strength may drop slightly. Goal is to lose <10% on any lift. If a major lift drops 20%+, that's a flag — recovery, sleep, or food is the issue. Strength loss in late cut is not failure. Strength gain in late cut is not realistic. Maintenance is the win. RED FLAGS IN TRAINING
Pull the cord and check in with me if: Same weight feels harder 3 sessions running on the same exercise
Joint pain (not muscle soreness) lasting >5 days
Resting HR up 10+ bpm pre-workout consistently
Can't complete planned sets at usual weight 2 weeks in a row
Sleep dropping below 6 hours despite trying These usually mean: insufficient recovery, food too low, or sleep is broken. Fixable, but only if flagged early.
Sunday is fully off. No steps target, no cardio, no lift. Walk if you naturally want to, don't if you don't. Genuine rest day.
Implications I'm flagging, not negotiating: Step target becomes a 6-day average, not 7. To hit a weekly volume equivalent to 12k × 7 = 84k steps, you'd need ~14k across 6 active days. To hit 15k × 7 = 105k weekly, you'd need ~17.5k across 6 days. That's a lot. More realistic: just lower the weekly total slightly and accept the math. Updated step targets (6-day average): Wk 1-2: 9,000/day (6 days)
Wk 3-6: 11,000/day
Wk 7-17: 12,000-13,000/day
Wk 18-24: 14,000/day
Wk 25-35: 15,000/day Sunday food matters. Lower activity day = lower TDEE that day. Don't compensate by eating more "because it's a rest day" — keep the 1800 cal floor steady. If anything, Sunday is when adherence wobbles for most people (family meals, social context, less structure). Pre-decide what you eat on Sundays.
Refeed should NOT land on Sunday. Refeeds are tied to your heaviest training day to refill glycogen. Sunday has no training. Pick Tuesday (Lower A) or Friday (Lower B) as your refeed day.
Lifting program stays 4x/week. Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri is your default. Wednesday becomes the cardio/active recovery day. Saturday is the second cardio day later in the cut.
Sundays are also planning days. Use the rest as part of the system: Sunday evening, glance at the week ahead — meals planned, training scheduled, any social events to navigate. 10 minutes of planning prevents a derailed week. Recovery Activities WHAT THEY ACTUALLY DO (AND DON'T)
Sauna: ✅ Cardiovascular adaptation (real, modest)
✅ Mental relaxation, sleep quality bump if used in evening
✅ Some evidence for heat shock proteins, recovery
✅ Feels good
❌ Does NOT meaningfully burn fat. The "calories burned in sauna" figures are mostly water weight returning the second you drink fluid.
❌ Does NOT detox. Your liver and kidneys do that. Sweat is mostly water + trace electrolytes. Cold plunge / cold shower: ✅ Mood / alertness boost (real, short-lived)
✅ May help with DOMS perception
✅ Mental discipline practice
❌ Does NOT meaningfully boost metabolism. The "brown fat activation" calorie burn is real but tiny — ~50-100 cal max.
❌ Cold immediately post-lifting blunts muscle protein synthesis and hypertrophy. This matters for you. HOW TO INCORPORATE — RULES
Cold plunge / cold showers: Never within 4-6 hours of lifting. This is the big one. Cold exposure post-workout reduces muscle adaptation by ~15-20% in studies. You're cutting hard and trying to preserve muscle — don't sabotage the lifting. Save cold for non-training mornings or evenings.
Best times: morning on rest days (Wed, Sat, Sun), or evenings on lift days after enough time has passed.
Duration: 2-5 minutes is plenty. Longer doesn't help more. Sauna: Post-lifting is fine. Heat exposure doesn't blunt hypertrophy the way cold does. Some evidence it may help via heat shock proteins.
Hydrate aggressively. You're already on 1800 cal in a deficit. Sauna sweat = electrolyte loss. Use your Re-lyte bottle. Don't sauna fasted in the morning if you're already dehydrated overnight.
Don't sauna immediately before sleep. Core temp needs to drop for sleep onset. Sauna 2-3 hours before bed = good. Sauna right before bed = disrupts sleep.
Duration: 15-25 min is the sweet spot. Hotter ≠ better. Multiple short visits with cool-down between is better than one long burn. WHERE THEY FIT IN YOUR WEEK Cold on lift days only if 4+ hours after the session. CONTRAST (HOT/COLD ALTERNATING):
Popular but evidence is weak. If you enjoy it, fine. Same rule applies — not within 4 hours of lifting if cold is involved. ONE WARNING SPECIFIC TO YOUR CUT:
In late phases (week 25+), you'll be: Lower body fat = less insulation
Lower calories = lower thermogenesis
Higher risk of cold intolerance as a warning sign If you start finding cold plunges genuinely unbearable when they used to be fine, or if sauna leaves you dizzy/nauseous when it used to feel good — that's data. Could be electrolyte depletion, could be the early signs of metabolic adaptation we're watching for. Tell me when it changes, don't push through.
Also: don't sauna or plunge on diet break / refeed days if you don't want to. The body's already managing a lot of fluid shifts those days. Unnecessary stressor.
Plan Overview
THE PLAN — your one-page reference TIMELINE Start: Monday 4 May 2026 Finish: 1 January 2027 (35 weeks) 114kg → target 84-85kg NUTRITION BY PHASE Refeed: ~3000 cal, mostly carbs, same protein. Not a cheat day. Pick Tuesday or Friday (Lower day). Diet breaks (non-negotiable): 5 days at maintenance in week 10 and week 22. TRAINING (Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri, Sun fully off) Wk 1-2: 3x/week full body (Mon/Wed/Fri), all machines + few free weight basics
Wk 3-32: 4x/week upper-lower split (Upper A / Lower A / Upper B / Lower B)
Wk 18+: Add intensity techniques (drop sets, rest-pause) on isolations
Wk 33-35: Dial back volume, protect lifts Reps: 6-12 range. 1-2 RIR most sets, last set to failure on machines. Track every session. Notebook or notes app. Don't build an app. CARDIO + STEPS (6-day average, Sunday off) Zone-2 = incline treadmill walk or bike, conversational pace. Wed + Sat. SUPPLEMENTS / DAILY Metformin per prescription (talk to GP about cut)
Multivitamin with food
Creatine 5g/day (start this weekend)
Re-lyte 1.5 scoops + creatine in 40oz Owala, sip pre/during/post workout
Plain water 3-4L/day on top
Caffeine pre-workout (strategic, not all-day)
Fiber 30g/day from food NON-NEGOTIABLES Track everything in MyFitnessPal for 35 weeks
Saturday morning weigh-ins, 7-day rolling average
Lift heavy, progressive overload
7+ hours sleep
No alcohol for 35 weeks
Pre-decide social event strategy
Hold both diet breaks (week 10, week 22)
Sunday full rest (already locked) WARNING SIGNS — flag if 2+ for 2 weeks running RHR drops 10+ bpm below baseline
Sleep crash
Cold intolerance
Mood persistently low
Training performance collapse → Action: 5-day diet break + step calories up 100. Don't push through. ADJUSTMENT RULES Weight stalls 2 weeks: +1000 steps OR -100 cal, pick one
Life explodes (exams, placement): maintenance week. No compensatory deficit after.
Going faster than projected: stay the course. Don't accelerate. THIS WEEKEND Saturday AM: baseline weigh-in (post-bathroom, pre-food) Photos: front, side, back. Save dated folder. MyFitnessPal set up, Monday meals pre-logged Groceries: chicken, mince, eggs, Greek yogurt, oats, rice, frozen veg, salad, protein powder, protein milk, Zilch, electrolytes, creatine Lifting log set up (notebook or notes app — not an app build) Creatine started Saturday GP appointment booked re: metformin + cut Sunday: write Monday's training plan, bed by 10:30pm Find a photo of yourself at 83kg if you have one — phone home screen CHECK-IN RHYTHM Monday nights: quick numbers — weight, cals, protein, training Y/N, sleep hours
Saturday mornings: weekly weigh-in + 7-day average + training summary + adherence honest assessment
Ad hoc: real obstacles, social event planning, troubleshooting REVERSE DIET (Jan onwards) Wk 1: 2000 → Wk 2: 2200 → Wk 3: 2400 → Wk 4: 2500 (new maintenance at 84kg)

headers.heading: A refeed = one planned high-calorie day per week, eaten at or slightly above maintenance, with the extra calories coming primarily from carbs.

info_showCustomIcons.description: Show custom icons for files and folders

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
